characters.description: A returned traveler from Earth whose young body no longer holds the soulcrafter power he once possessed. He knows the Nine Worlds from an earlier life, conceals that history from those around him, and intends to rebuild with a more defensive soul home while pursuing an old betrayal.
characters.name: Theo

characters.description: A displaced soulcrafter overwhelmed by Tatian sunlight and repeated unwanted contact. Her frightened attack shows the danger of the cultural and sensory gulf separating the newly arrived refugees from their hosts.
characters.name: Ichili woman

characters.description: The Ichili woman stranded on Tatian after an interworld disruption. She accepts a light-blocking blindfold and Theo's help, then agrees to seek soulcrafter training while waiting for a relative who may be able to retrieve her.
characters.name: Fiyu

characters.description: The name Theo gives Fiyu and the Tatian hosts while concealing his past. Under this identity he proposes a training arrangement for the stranded travelers and begins forming a practical alliance with Fiyu.
characters.name: Jake

characters.description: A stranded stone-bodied scholar who shapes part of himself into a plate, allowing Theo to make Fiyu a protective blindfold. He has never left his own world before the disruption deposits him on Tatian.
characters.name: Arbai Mundine

characters.description: A threatening returnee from Earth who claims to have reached the Dominion tier before dying to a powerful demon. He warns Theo that repeated returns attract abnormal danger, advises distrust of other Earth travelers, and takes an evident interest in the risks surrounding them.
characters.name: Magnafor

characters.description: The Arbai Mundine scholar traveling with the displaced group. A capable stone shaper, he agrees to make Theo a light spear carrying force and piercing properties even though Theo has not yet regained soulcrafter power.
characters.name: Navim

characters.description: A Tatian farm guard nearing ascension who fought during the village defense. He directs the stranded travelers toward Mufuru, where they can exchange public service for training and compete for valuable materials.
characters.name: Famage

characters.description: Famage's uncle and the head of Mufuru Landguard Village. He oversees the training program for displaced soulcrafters and its guarded stores, while his concealed soul home suggests power well beyond the rank he displays.
characters.name: Nanjuma

characters.description: A Tatian training-family leader who fights with a forked, stone-set staff. She chooses Fiyu, Theo, and Kuber for her group and aims to win the school's rewards so she can advance her own soul home.
characters.name: Nauda

characters.description: A Tatian student selected for Nauda's training family. His soul home has an unusually broad foundation, making him a potentially useful addition to a group preparing for instruction and competition.
characters.name: Kuber

characters.description: A powerful stone-bodied scholar at Navim's school who possesses a weirkey. He secures treatment for his critically damaged student and offers the stranded companions passage back to Tatian in return for future accounts of worlds beyond the Nine.
characters.name: Navim's elder master

characters.description: An authority-tier landguard who questions Nanjuma after the destruction at Mufuru. Claiming that arrivals from beyond the Nine attract demons, the investigator represents a wider effort to identify and control displaced outsiders.
characters.name: Landguard investigator

characters.description: The independent ruler of Nlukoko, a commercial city outside ordinary landguard control. Nauda warns that he is stronger than a ruler and that antagonizing him would be exceptionally dangerous.
characters.name: Lord of Nlukoko

characters.description: A small blue, white-haired, adult-seeming humanoid whom Theo rescues during the Nlukoko demon breach. She speaks in an unusual manner, attaches herself to Theo, and follows the travelers through the gate despite their uncertainty about her nature.
characters.name: Senka

characters.description: The Dusan cover identity Theo presents to a local family leader after intervening in a court dispute. The name supports his attempt to navigate family politics without exposing his true history.
characters.name: Bartolo Ein of Fythe

characters.description: A noble hunter who leads an advanced sleigh team during the great rainhorn hunt. He offers to double the group's reward if they take at least half as many rainhorns as his team, with a duel against Theo as the penalty for failure.
characters.name: Dusan rival

characters.description: An archcrafter serving the Dusan family force that pursues Theo's group across the northern wasteland. He carries a pike and joins the attempt to corner the travelers while a ruler-tier sublime beast threatens both sides.
characters.name: Pike-wielding archcrafter

characters.description: An erratic authority who abruptly replaces his house's recruitment trial with a test of endurance under overwhelming pressure. He strikes Nauda down after she outlasts nearly every applicant, then dismisses all candidates.
characters.name: Red-bannered house authority

characters.description: The administrator who evaluates the travelers during House Blacksilver's soul-home trial and becomes their working contact after recruitment. The official offers merit-based contracts and explains the costly route to the house's available chasm invitations.
characters.name: House Blacksilver official

characters.description: An elderly Dusan ruler who arrives with an Anguadan group and approaches Theo after another member disappears from view.
characters.name: Elderly Dusan ruler

characters.description: An elderly ruler leading the family that has followed Theo to Norro Yorthin. He treats Theo's knowledge of a historical Dusan family as dangerous, attacks when Theo cannot satisfy him, and presses a vendetta that results in a delayed formal duel.
characters.name: Dusan family head

characters.description: An authority with a personal interest in breaking Nauda's resolve. Before Theo's duel, he humiliates and threatens her, then stakes a chasm invitation on an insulting wager that Theo will lose.
characters.name: House Crimson authority

characters.description: A senior House Crimson authority who responds to the younger authority's treatment of Nauda by giving Nauda and Fiyu a potent sublime spice as an apology.
characters.name: Older House Crimson authority

characters.description: The elder who speaks for the expelled Dusan household after its champion loses. He explains that inquiry into a family destroyed about a century earlier has repeatedly led to death, demons, and renewed destruction.
characters.name: Dusan family elder
